changelog: security: v0.9.12.2 — authorized penetration test + remediation. All CRITICAL and HIGH findings fixed and each E2E-verified against a live lab. CRITICAL: (C-1) the VNC/termproxy console no longer hands the cluster-wide PVE session ticket (PVEAuthCookie = effectively root on pve:8006) to the browser — the standalone WS proxy now fetches it server-side via cluster-creds / ws-token-validate, the browser only ever sees the per-console scoped termproxy ticket; (C-2/M-2) V2P migration's esxi_datastore / esxi_vm_dir are validated against a strict single-name allowlist at the API boundary AND re-validated at the v2p chokepoint, plus shlex.quote on the sshfs mount paths — closes a root command-injection on the PVE node reachable by a default migrate-permission user. HIGH: (H-1/H-2) every console path (VNC route, in-process + standalone WS proxies, termproxy ticket) now enforces cluster access AND per-VM user_can_access_vm('vm.console') — previously only the vm.console permission was checked, so a console user could reach any VM on any cluster/tenant; (H-3) API-token perms are evaluated against the token's floored role, not the owner's — an admin-owned viewer/user token no longer inherits admin on perms-guarded routes; (H-4) web-push send re-validates the endpoint host server-side + refuses redirects; (H-5) the plain→SQLCipher migration's plaintext DB backup (every cluster password in clear) is now securely shredded on startup past a 1h window (PEGAPROX_PLAIN_BACKUP_RETENTION_S) instead of retained indefinitely next to the keystore; (H-6) request bodies are capped per-request so a chunked Transfer-Encoding body can no longer bypass the size limit and OOM the process. MEDIUM: (M-3) cluster-group endpoints read the correct tenant key (was user.get('tenant'), always None → cross-tenant group/metrics leak) and the manual cross-cluster balance + lb-history now enforce group ownership (was a BOLA that could trigger live VM migrations on another tenant's group); (M-7) alert webhook channels (Slack/Discord/Teams/ntfy/generic) now pass the SSRF guard before sending + refuse redirects, and the channel-test endpoint no longer leaks the upstream HTTP status (port-scan oracle) — internal targets opt-in via alert_webhook_allow_private; (M-8/M-9) ACME re-validates every directory-supplied follow-on URL and ACME/SIEM/site-recovery outbound calls no longer follow redirects to internal hosts. OIDC was deliberately left untouched. UI: Corporate Layout polish within its flat Clarity aesthetic — theme-aware metric/threshold colors (light mode now correct), keyboard focus ring on toolbar buttons, fade-in on action dropdowns, symmetric property-grid padding, flat (non-gradient) light-mode modal header. CI: Docker image builds only on a released version tag (not every main push); release-image apt-poll retries raised 120→150; the LXC template now also ships as pegaprox-latest-lxc-amd64.tar.xz so releases/latest/download/ always resolves to the newest build. Scaling/stability hardening from the 30-cluster/100-node audit carried over from the Testing line (off-hub metrics reads/writes, broadcast throttling, watched-cluster gating, bounded caches, syslog receiver DoS fix + UI toggle).

hotfix: v0.9.12.1 — fixes #514 (jostrasser) PBS-host placeholder allowlist regression. The Aikido SSRF-hardening patch (PR #475) that landed in v0.9.12.0 shipped `_validate_pbs_host` with `allowed_domains = ['example.com']` as a literal placeholder. Effect: at boot every customer-configured PBS was rejected with 'Invalid or disallowed host', and connect/test endpoints returned 400 'Invalid PBS host' for any non-example.com input. Allowlist removed in pegaprox/core/pbs.py — the format regex (`^[a-zA-Z0-9.\-:]+$`) is the actual SSRF defence here: it refuses scheme prefixes, paths, quoting and shell metas, so a malicious host value can't redirect requests off-host. PBS hosts are per-customer config (`pbs.internal`, `10.x.x.x`) — operator-side FQDN allowlists can't work in this surface. 13/13 smoke cases pass (FQDN / short / IPv4 / IPv6 / hyphens accepted; empty / scheme / paths / shell-metas / whitespace rejected). Codebase sweep done — no other placeholder-allowlist patterns elsewhere (`api/vms.py:_validate_host` and `utils/sanitization.py:validate_hostname` are pure format checks; `.ssh_ws_server.py:allowed_hosts` is built dynamically per-request from cluster_host + node_ips; `url_security.py` scheme-allowlist has sensible defaults).

release: v0.9.12.0 — vacation-R3 wrap-up (25.–30.05.). Headlines: (#509 davinkevin) SQLCipher mlock spam silenced in rootless containers — new PEGAPROX_CIPHER_MEMORY_SECURITY env var with `auto` (default; heuristic: euid + RLIMIT_MEMLOCK), `on`, `off`. Encryption-at-rest unchanged, just the in-memory page-cache mlock() is skipped when it would fail. (#510 davinkevin) per-node status line demoted info → debug — saves ~720 lines/h of routine metrics on a 5×6-node fleet. (gyptazy PR #429) ACME DNS-01 challenge support with RFC 2136 dynamic-update (BIND/PowerDNS/Knot) — `hmac-sha512` default, full algorithm matrix. (gyptazy PR #508) PVE node subscription management under Datacenter — set/refresh/delete keys per node + cluster-wide aggregator; subscription key masked in the aggregator response (cluster.view exposes only the last 4 chars; full key still on the per-node admin endpoint). (#413 blackshocks layer 5) Site Recovery planned + emergency failover now re-runnable from completed/failed (previously the atomic `WHERE status='ready'` transition rejected every retry with a misleading 'concurrent failover' message). CodeAnt batch: API-token role-refresh no longer auto-escalates a viewer token to its admin owner's current role; WS-token validate cross-checks against `cluster_id` from URL; SSH-WS host overrides allow-listed against cluster_host + node_ips; vmware api_get/post/delete path-traversal guard; OIDC test endpoint pre-validates authority URL before composing discovery; subscription-key masking on cluster.view aggregator; useEffect clusterId-dep on Datacenter lazy sections (ceph/metric-server/subscriptions) — no more stale node rows on cluster switch. Aikido batch (May 30): 3rd-party action SHA pinning across all three workflows (10 actions, latest stable in current major), template-injection fix on release-tag interpolation, Debian cloud-image SHA512 verify before virt-customize (#509 ack'd this gap separately on main). Plain-JSON config fallback removed from 5 load paths — encrypted DB is now the single source of truth; encrypted .enc legacy fallback in core/config.py kept (defense-in-depth, no plain-text spill). UX: CPU type dropdown sorted host/max first, rest alphabetical case-insensitive across VM Create + Config; cluster worldmap zoom-wheel no longer floods console with passive-listener preventDefault warnings; first-run setup wizard replaces the hardcoded pegaprox/admin default (NOT_INITIALIZED state on /api/auth/login when no admin exists). Alert email payloads now html.escape user-controlled fields (alert name, target, message); audit log strips CR/LF/U+2028/U+2029 from user/action/details/cluster fields. ACME directory URL behind sanitize_outbound_url. version.json update_files now lists web/src/worldmap.js + web/assets/world-countries.svg (caught by post-release audit, would have left customer updates with a 404 on the new feature).

release: v0.9.11 — vacation-mode batch (R2: 21.–24.05.) shipped to Testing for stabilisation before tagging. Headlines: (#444 surreal70) per-node auth circuit-breaker — runaway 401 loops on a misconfigured node no longer turn into a slow-DoS for the rest of the cluster; the breaker opens after N consecutive auth failures, half-open probe re-arms cleanly. (#412 SeeJayEmm) OIDC discovery flow now tolerates issuers without /.well-known/openid-configuration documents and falls back to issuer-relative endpoint guesses with a clear UI warning. (#436 aalandez) snapshot-schedule race when two clusters fire at the same wall-clock minute — scheduler now de-dupes per (cluster_id, schedule_id) under a lock. (#438 crcro) ESXi migration partial-failures stop the loop cleanly instead of half-attaching the remaining disks. (#446 hugobugomugo) API-token rotation no longer corrupts the token table when the user is mid-session. (#448 DarmokNoob) cross-cluster LXC replication clone uses `hostname=` instead of `name=` (LXC schema rejects `name`, QEMU still accepts it). PVE 9.2 coexistence: `find_migration_candidate` now skips VMs flagged `auto-rebalance=1` so PegaProx LB and PVE-CRS don't fight each other; pre-9.2 behaviour unchanged. `scheduling=` payload key stripped from `/cluster/options` on PVE 9.2+ (key was removed in 9.2; older PVE versions still accept). Per-node SSH/API circuit-breaker + host-skip cache: dead-node UI hang reduced from 30s/poll to ~4s. 29 i18n keys added (CRS / token-rotate / OIDC). New UI: Capacity Outlook on the corporate dashboard now reads `/insights/forecast` (linear-regression + R²-gated) instead of the WMA-current endpoint, presenting a one-glance cluster-wide forecast (closest-ETA value + worst-metric subtitle). PR #445 (gyptazy/credativ) VM API route shorthand merged.

hotfix: v0.9.10.3 — 14 customer-reported fixes batched over the four-day vacation-mode patrol week (15.05.–18.05.). Headlines: (#417, tgmct/elektronen) keystore-permission boot-loop on fresh installs — deploy.sh now writes /etc/pegaprox/secret.key as 0640 root:pegaprox so the service can read its own key, with on-upgrade repair-bump from 0600/0400 → 0640 for existing v0.9.10/.1/.2 installs; pegaprox/core/keystore.py `_enforce_perms` accepts 0600 OR 0640, rejects anything looser (raises, no silent skip). Plus deploy.sh + update.sh now honour `PEGAPROX_BRANCH` env-var so install-from-Testing actually clones Testing instead of always main. (#411, crcro) v2p disk-copy: surface bash-script stdout+stderr on rc≠0 so 'Copy failed!' becomes actionable; fix chmod literal-string bug. (#412, SeeJayEmm) OIDC `oidc_allow_private_ip` opt-in config knob for internal IdPs at 10.x/192.168.x (metadata endpoints still blocked) plus matching UI toggle with 7-language translations. (#413, blackshocks) Site Recovery boot-recovery sweep: stuck `site_recovery_events` rows with status='running' get flipped to 'aborted' at app start with `{reason: pegaprox_restart}` details, stuck plans go from running/testing back to failed so the state machine re-arms cleanly. (#414, KowMangler) cross-cluster migration pre-flight warning for `unused*` references that PVE keeps after a Detach — surfaces the disk/ISO leftovers that would otherwise blow up the migration with 'storage does not support vm images'. (#415, KowMangler) dashboard cpu_percent/mem_percent enrichment on /api/clusters/<id>/nodes (was missing → cross-cluster-migration target-node dropdown showed empty %). (#416, KowMangler) Clone VM target-node dropdown stabilised via useMemo+empty-deps snapshot at mount so the auto-refresh poll doesn't reshuffle entries while you're picking one. (#398+#400, mihsu81) PVE → XCP-ng migration: qemu-img convert `/dev/stdout` → `-` to bypass the ftruncate-EINVAL bail, and qcow2 detection now matches `.qcow2` suffix alone so qcow2-on-LVM block devices stop being misidentified as raw. (#419, SpyrosPsarras) Node Network In/Out columns now use /nodes/<n>/rrddata instead of /cluster/resources?type=node (the latter never returns netin/netout for nodes, only for VMs — was always 0). (#385, maxilee) VM bridge dropdown drops OVSIntPort entries — internal OVS access ports aren't bridges, PVE web UI doesn't show them either. (#365, j0c00) Generate-MAC button now honours Datacenter → Options → MAC address prefix (BC:24:11 etc.) instead of always rolling 02:xx. (#357, SeeJayEmm) New env vars `PEGAPROX_LOG_LEVEL` / `PEGAPROX_FILE_LOG_LEVEL` / `PEGAPROX_DISABLE_FILE_LOG` for operators shipping logs to a central collector. Plus: support-bundle now falls back to `journalctl -u pegaprox` when no pegaprox.log file exists (most systemd / LXC-appliance installs route straight to journald). Workaround for existing v0.9.10.x installs hitting the #417 boot-loop before this patch: `sudo chmod 640 /etc/pegaprox/secret.key && sudo systemctl restart pegaprox`.

hotfix: v0.9.10.2 — Corporate-layout button regression sweep + OIDC test endpoint hardening. Triggered by four customer reports filed within hours of the v0.9.10 release (#406 disk-resize translation, #407 Ceph monitor add, #408 Ceph pool delete, #409 generic 'buttons broken'). Pattern was systematic across the 1.4k-line Corporate UI: 27 user-action click handlers had `} catch (e) {}` silently swallowing every error path — modals stayed open with no toast, destructive POST/DELETE returned success-shaped UI even when PVE rejected the call. All 27 patched to surface either PVE's actual error message or the network error: Ceph pool create/delete, monitor add/delete, OSD action/scrub × 2 surfaces, CephFS delete, 10 firewall option toggles, firewall rule toggle, firewall alias/ipset CRUD × 4, plus 3 ipset entry handlers. Plus hardcoded German strings in the disk-resize modal (`Aktuelle Groesse:`, `Vergroessern`) replaced with translation keys (new `currentSize` key in DE/EN/FR/ES/PT/KO/IT, reused existing `resize`). On the OIDC side: `oidc_test_connection` crashed 500/empty-body when `get_oidc_endpoints()` returned None (SSRF guard rejected a malformed authority/issuer URL) — exact symptom of #188 gerwin221's 'Network error' after v0.9.10.1 upgrade. Fix: `get_oidc_endpoints` now returns structured dict with `_error` key instead of None; test endpoint checks for it and surfaces clean 'Endpoint Resolution' step with actionable detail instead of crashing. Added `oidc_issuer` as alias for `oidc_authority` in config override so API clients using the OIDC-spec term get accepted.

hotfix: v0.9.10.1 — Aikido-driven post-release cleanup. Fixed two critical SAST findings in pegaprox/cli/migrate_db.py — table names from sqlite_master were embedded into SELECT COUNT(*) via f-string; replaced with an identifier whitelist + escaped double-quoting (`_safe_quote_ident()`). Practical exploitability was nil (the table names come from the DB schema, not HTTP input) but the f-string SQL pattern is an unconditional defense-in-depth no-go. Plus dependency floor bumps to clear the rest of the Aikido critical/high CVEs: urllib3 2.5.0→2.7.0 (CVE-2026-44431 critical), werkzeug 3.0.3→3.1.6 (CVE-2024-49767 DOS, CVE-2024-49766 path traversal, CVE-2026-27199, AIKIDO-2024-10410 weak crypto). Open: 12 SAST 'SSRF via constructed URL' findings on Proxmox/ESXi/XAPI passthrough calls — each needs case-by-case review to determine whether the URL component is genuinely attacker-controlled or already constrained server-side; tracked for the next sweep.

release: v0.9.10 — Full-DB SQLCipher encryption, multi-tier master-key loader, LXC dedicated terminal, syslog cluster-scoping, Corporate-layout enhancements, dependency floor bumps (Flask 3 / gevent 25 / werkzeug 3 / urllib3 2.5 / paramiko 4 / sqlcipher3-binary 0.6 / pyasn1 0.6.3 / setuptools 78.1 / zipp 3.19 / h11 0.16), 42-site reflected-content sanitizer on Proxmox-passthrough error paths, Docker HEALTHCHECK retuned for the in-process migration window.

Feature: LXC dedicated text terminal — direct `pct enter` access via the existing SSH-websocket transport, surfaced from both the corporate layout VM modal and the modern dashboard. New backend route `/api/clusters/<cluster_id>/vms/<node>/lxc/<vmid>/termwebsocket` reuses the SSH WS auth path so token / password posture matches the existing node-shell behaviour. Translation key `lxcTerminal` shipped in DE/EN/FR/ES/PT/KO.

Feature: Corporate-layout enhancements across the dashboard / VM modals / config tabs — refined surface tokens, density, and information hierarchy in the vCenter-style chrome (≈1.4k lines of new UI sources). Backwards-compatible with the modern (Proxmox-orange) layout — switch via user prefs.

Feature: cluster-scoped syslog viewer (#387, PR #399, contributed by @gyptazy, sponsored by credativ GmbH). New Settings → Syslog Server tab with a toggle; when enabled and a cluster is selected in the dashboard, the Syslog event list filters to log rows whose hostname matches one of that cluster's hosts/nodes. Default off — pre-existing 'all syslog rows' behaviour is preserved. Hostname matching is robust: handles URL forms, IPv4/IPv6 with or without port, FQDN short-name matching, and falls back gracefully when a manager can't enumerate its nodes. Audit log captures both enable/disable flips under the `settings.syslog` action.

Security: reflected-content hardening on 42 Proxmox-passthrough error paths across pegaprox/api/{datacenter,vms,storage,static_files}.py — every `jsonify({'error': response.text})` now routes through parse_pve_error() which extracts only the structured `message` field from the PVE JSON envelope, truncates to 500 chars, html-escapes the output, and falls back to a generic 'Proxmox API error' string when the upstream body looks HTML-shaped. Closes Snyk Code's CWE-79 cluster on SDN endpoints (10 highs around create_/delete_/apply_sdn_subnet, get_/create_sdn_controller). The actual reflected-XSS exploitability was already low because Flask's jsonify sets Content-Type: application/json and modern browsers don't execute scripts in JSON, but defense-in-depth makes the trace clean and protects future code paths that might serve the same string as text/html.

Security: dependency floor bump to clear Snyk / pip-audit findings — flask-cors 6.0.0 (3 CVEs incl. case-sensitivity Origin allowlist bypass CVSS 8.7), gevent 25.4.1 (CVSS 9.3 trusting HTTP permission methods + CVSS 8.3 race condition + CVSS 6.9 request smuggling), werkzeug 3.0.3 (CVE-2024-34069 debugger pin RCE, transitive via Flask), Flask 3.0.3 (so werkzeug 3 is actually pulled), urllib3 2.5.0 (CVE-2025-50181 open redirect), paramiko 4.0.0 (drops legacy DSSKey — our existing getattr(paramiko, 'DSSKey', None) wrappers degrade cleanly), h11 0.16.0 (request smuggling CVSS 9.3, transitive via wsproto), plus explicit pins for pyasn1 ≥ 0.6.3, setuptools ≥ 78.1.1, zipp ≥ 3.19.1 (transitive vulns that scanners flag even when the resolved version was already safe). Verified: no use of Flask-3-removed APIs (before_first_request, JSONEncoder, _app_ctx_stack), no use of paramiko.DSSKey without a guard. The running 0.9.9.x venvs were already on these versions; the floors-bump is so fresh installs and scanners agree.

Security: Full-DB encryption via SQLCipher (AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA512, format v4) is now the default on Linux x86_64. The first boot after the update auto-detects a plain pegaprox.db and migrates it in-process: copy → sqlcipher_export → per-table row-count verify → atomic rename. A timestamped .plain.bak.<ts> is retained so operators can verify the encrypted DB before manually shred-deleting. The runtime falls back to plain SQLite + Fernet field-encryption when the sqlcipher3-binary wheel is unavailable (ARM, macOS, Windows). The master key is resolved by a multi-tier loader (env var → systemd LoadCredentialEncrypted → /etc/pegaprox/secret.key → ~/.config → legacy config dir) so backups of config/ no longer carry the decryption key alongside the encrypted data. Fresh installs via deploy.sh now place the key at /etc/pegaprox/secret.key (chmod 0600 root:pegaprox) instead of next to the DB. Field-level Fernet stays in place so platforms without sqlcipher3 still protect cluster passwords, TOTP secrets, OIDC client secrets, and API tokens. PEGAPROX_DISABLE_AUTO_ENCRYPT=1 opt-out for operators who want to take their own pre-encrypt backup first. See docs/SECURITY.md for the threat-model, recovery story, and the systemd-creds TPM2-bound key setup.

Hotfix: 19 files were on disk and shipped via the GitHub archive but missing from the per-file update_files manifest in version.json. When the updater fell through to the file-by-file fallback path (mirror 404, archive download glitch, etc.), pegaprox/api/dr_drill.py never landed and the service refused to start at import time (#383, mbunkus). Plus four example-plugin files (hello_world, proxmox-ha), eight image assets (the new theme-aware logo set + Expertize wordmark + sponsor variants), four doc files (SECURITY.md, Grafana dashboards), and one frontend dep (html2canvas) that were in the same blind spot. Going forward, a pre-release CI check diffs the on-disk tree against update_files and fails if anything is unlisted.

Hotfix: OIDC / Entra admin re-authentication for sensitive ops was incorrectly requiring TOTP in v0.9.9.1, but PegaProx blocks OIDC users from enrolling TOTP at /api/auth/2fa/setup (the IdP handles MFA for OIDC). Net effect: OIDC admins were locked out of any re-auth-gated operation. Replaced with a three-tier ladder: WebAuthn proof (preferred; works regardless of auth_source), TOTP if the user happens to have one (ex-local-converted), or session-validity acceptance with a loud audit log entry recommending WebAuthn enrolment. Proper OIDC `prompt=login` re-auth is tracked separately

Security: CSRF Origin matcher rewritten to use urllib.parse instead of string startswith() — closes a suffix-confusion vector (Origin: https://pegaprox.com.attacker.com would have slipped through), now scheme-agnostic so reverse-proxy setups without X-Forwarded-Proto forwarding stop hitting false 'CSRF validation failed' errors (#382). Hardened against Python's urlparse normalising tabs in scheme, FTP/gopher schemes with same hostname, userinfo confusion (http://evil@self), and explicit port mismatches
Security: XML parsing in pegaprox/core/xcpng.py switched from stdlib xml.etree to defusedxml — closes XXE / billion-laughs / external-entity expansion in iSCSI probe and RRD payloads
Security: paramiko floor bumped to 3.4.0 (CVE-2023-48795 Terrapin) and urllib3 to 2.0.7 (CVE-2023-43804 cookie redirect leak)

Security: OIDC / Entra admin re-authentication step-up now uses WebAuthn proof when registered (preferred), falls back to TOTP for ex-local-converted users, and accepts on session-validity with an audit-log warning when neither is enrolled. Replaces the buggy v0.9.9.1 behaviour that hard-required TOTP and locked OIDC admins out

Security: Defense-in-depth refactor of the public Status Page and Client Portal plugin HTMLs — strict CSP, single setHTMLSafe() audit point, Number()-cast on all numeric CSS attribute interpolations to close a residual CSS-attribute injection vector

Plugin system: list_plugins() now exposes has_frontend and frontend_route from manifest.json so the dashboard can render a plugin tab without core changes per plugin (#381). Strict server-side route validation rejects external URLs, control characters, query/fragment injection, parent-segment traversal, and paths into other plugins' namespaces. Generic plugin frontend tab in the dashboard with sandboxed iframe (no allow-top-navigation, encodeURIComponent on theme/cluster params). Manifest can declare relative form 'ui' or full path /api/plugins/<id>/api/...; everything else is rejected

Security headers: X-Frame-Options relaxed from DENY to SAMEORIGIN and CSP frame-ancestors from 'none' to 'self' to enable the plugin frontend iframe contract — cross-origin clickjacking remains blocked

UX: corporate disconnect / offline banner now shows the affected cluster and node names instead of just a count, with truncation past three names ('+N more') to avoid overflow at scale (#380). Translated into all 7 supported languages

Theme-aware logo switching: PegaProx pegasus mark in the sidebar / settings / login screen now picks light or dark variant based on the active layout (Modern dark, Corporate dark, Corporate light). PDF export uses the dark-pegasus variant for white-paper print
VNC console: fix for PVE 9.1.8/9.1.9 'invalid Connection header: keep-alive' on VM console open (#352). Lenient WS opening-handshake recovery restores the Upgrade token when intermediaries strip it
VNC console: single-vncproxy mode for PVE 9.1.x — JS-issued ticket+port now passed through to the WS handler so noVNC's RFB password matches PVE's per-call random VNC password (issue #352 follow-up)
VNC console: optional Stable VNC Mode (AES-256-GCM around RFB frames) for environments with TLS-inspection / EDR byte-mangling. Browser-side opt-in in user prefs
VNC console: optional SSH-tunnel mode (per-cluster `vnc_tunnel: true`) routes the PegaProx↔PVE leg through SSH — defeats inspection on the second leg. Multi-user safe, persistent client per cluster
VNC console: HTTP-polling fallback transport that auto-engages when the WS leg is hard-blocked. Same crypto, plain HTTPS POST/GET, lazy ticket fetch (no double vncproxy)

Backup task edit: PUT /cluster/backup/{id} now strips read-only fields (next-run, last-run-status, …) and converts dict-shaped fields (prune-backups, fleecing, performance) to PVE's property-string format — fixes 'Parameter verification failed' when editing a job created in PVE (#338)
Replication: VM Configure tab now shows actual last_sync / state / fail_count instead of 'Never'. Per-node /replication endpoint is now merged with the cluster-wide job list (#333)
Datacenter → Replication: clearer hint that the dialog only does intra-cluster replication; cross-cluster replication is via VM → Configure → Replication → Cross-Cluster (#320)
Pool member assignment: idempotent 'already a pool member' path now returns success, surfaces real PVE errors instead of generic 'Failed to add VM to pool' (#349)
Site Recovery readiness check: free-memory math fixed — was reading the wrong field names and warned <2GB free even when 13G+ was available (#350)
LDAP / AD: nested-group expansion using LDAP_MATCHING_RULE_IN_CHAIN OID — Built-in Users / Domain Users style mappings now resolve to the right role instead of falling back to Viewer (#353)
Config backup / restore: LDAP-sourced users can now create backups by re-binding to the IdP for password verification instead of comparing against a non-existent local hash (#355)
Certificate management: hardcoded German placeholders replaced with translated strings — no more 'Zertifikat vorhanden' on the English UI (#354)
VM creation: ostype dropdown updated to match PVE 9.1's full list (added wvista / w2k8 / w2k3 / w2k / solaris, labels aligned with the Proxmox UI wording) (#358)
Audit/Compliance Report: 524 missing translation keys filled in across DE/EN/FR/ES/PT/IT — the report now renders fully in the user's language
User prefs: Stable VNC Mode toggle is now React-state-driven so the knob position updates immediately on click (small visual polish)
prev_changelog: PBS Reports tab: executive summary, inventory, gap analysis + PDF/PNG export (#273)
Rolling update: optional --with-local-disks evacuation, HA-rerouted migrations no longer false-fail (#330, #340)
Scheduled actions: name and vm_type actually persist now (#337)
Site Recovery: OVS bridges + SDN vnets show up in the network mapping dropdown (#329)
SSH reachability: corosync-VLAN setups now resolve the real mgmt IP for all node ops (#324)
Node Hardening: PDF/PNG export of CIS/Lynis/STIG/PegaProx audit reports
KSM Sharing visible in Node Summary, always shown (matches native PVE UI)
Corporate dashboard: single-node clusters show 'Standalone' badge instead of 'Quorum verloren' (#326)
Disk Create modal: dropdown click no longer dismisses the modal (#323)
